commonIssues: Root rot from overwatering or poor drainage — the most common failure mode
Leaf browning/edge burn from very dry air, drafts, or cold water
Leaf edge burn from excessive fertilizer
Leaf scorch in direct sun — keep in indirect light
Pests: mealybugs, aphids, scale, spider mites
Cold injury to foliage below ~7 C (45 F); keep above ~15 C (60 F) in winter
Contains calcium oxalate crystals — toxic to pets (dogs/cats) and irritating sap; keep out of reach
Becomes leggy with age as stems elongate

source.citation: Clemson University HGIC, 'Chinese Evergreen (Aglaonema) Care, Cultivation & Growing Guide' — low to moderate indirect light; keep soil moist but not wet, let top 1-2 in dry between waterings; optimal 68-80 F, sensitive below 50 F; soilless mix with extra humus; excessive fertilizer causes leaf edge burn; pests mealybugs/aphids/scale/mites; toxic to pets.

NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox, 'Aglaonema' — deep shade (<2 h direct sun) to partial shade (2-6 h); erect and bushy habit; keep mix moist spring-fall, reduce in winter; prefers 70-80 F daytime, intolerant of cold; calcium oxalate toxicity.

UF/IFAS publication FPS25/FP025, 'Aglaonema spp.: Chinese Evergreen' — grows in shade, leaf scorch in sun; let soil dry slightly a few days between waterings; foliage injury below 45 F; USDA zones 10B-11B; 1-3 ft tall/wide; suitable for containers; root rot from excess moisture.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, 'Aglaonema commutatum' — diffused sun or good indirect light; consistent moisture spring-fall, reduce early fall to late winter but never let soil fully dry; 60 F recommended winter minimum; well-drained peaty mix; 1-1.5 ft tall/wide; zones 10-12; rots if overwatered, leaves brown in dry air/drafts.

University of Illinois Extension, 'Favorite houseplants of a horticulturist' — fertilize aglaonema every four weeks with balanced houseplant fertilizer, every six weeks in winter; slow grower, rarely needs repotting; prefers slightly moist soil; brighter leaf color needs more light.

Confidence note: no authoritative source states a numeric watering interval; baseIntervalDays=7 is a conservative estimate derived from Clemson's 'let top 1-2 inches dry' guidance for a slow-growing indoor aroid. Container liters (4 L min ≈ 6-8 in pot) estimated from MOBOT/UF mature size of 1-3 ft for a compact, slow-growing plant. heatStressC=32 estimated from Clemson's 68-80 F optimum upper bound. Sun-hour values translate shade/indirect-light guidance (NCSU deep shade to 2-6 h partial shade) into the schema's hours/day.
